name: White Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald the milk and let it cool with water until tepid.
2. Dissolve 2 compressed yeast cakes in 1/2 cup lukewarm water and 1 tablespoon sugar.
3. Combine the yeast mixture with 1 pint of lukewarm water.
4. Gradually add flour to the mixture and knead into a large loaf until it is smooth but not too stiff.
5. Cover and let rise for 2 hours.
6. Knead down once more, cover, and let rise for another hour.
7. Shape into loaves and let rise for an additional hour, or until doubled in bulk.
8. Bake at 425°F for 15 minutes, then reduce to 350°F for the remainder of the time.
Will make 2 or 3 loaves. You can use water instead of milk, and add 3 medium-sized boiled and mashed potatoes if preferred.
